title: dacrycarpus cinctus
intro: Dacrycarpus cinctus, also known as the yellow kauri or swamp kauri, is a species of coniferous tree native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands, specifically in New Caledonia and Vanuatu. The medicinal properties of Dacrycarpus cinctus include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of using Dacrycarpus cinctus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and promote wound healing. The herbal preparations of Dacrycarpus cinctus include extracts, oils, and infusions that can be used to treat a range of ailments, often made by boiling the tree's resin or bark in water.
